meta.project_type: TypeScript Node.js CLI and code-generation/audit platform
meta.domain_hypothesis: A standalone CLI that audits or forms repositories, renders codebase-specific agent intelligence, exports harness surfaces, and optionally installs a GitHub-driven agent runtime.

lifecycle.sdlc_model: issue/branch -> focused implementation + regression test -> layered validation -> pull-request review -> changelog/docs update -> tag-only verified release

trigger_phrases: When changing CodebaseMap or audit schema contracts
When moving schema ownership across domain files
per_area_template_candidates.rationale: Schema structure, ordering, ownership, and golden compatibility form a specialized recurring workflow.

trigger_phrases: When changing provider-scoped state selection or migration
When changing crash recovery or transaction semantics
per_area_template_candidates.rationale: State changes require transaction, rollback, symlink, fingerprint, and legacy compatibility checks.

existing_planning_artifact.format_hint: Living roadmap under docs/roadmaps with staged FDE operating-system direction.

top_level_tree: src/ — TypeScript CLI and core runtime
tests/ — unit, integration, parity, security, and package tests

entry_points.role: npm executable entrypoint that loads the built CLI
source CLI entrypoint and command dispatcher

code_test_mirror.pattern: src/core feature areas are mirrored by tests/<area>/ and top-level *.test.ts files; scaffold has its own scaffold/tests suite

first_5_files_for_fresh_agent.why: Product boundary, supported command surface, repository layout, and operator workflow.
Runtime requirements, public bin entry, dependencies, build/test/typecheck commands.
Source command-line entrypoint and top-level dispatch.
Primary application seam behind the public CLI.
Canonical structured contracts for audit maps and artifact intents.

app_vs_agentic_layer.app_layer: src/cli.ts and src/core implement the published CLI; bin/agentify.js loads dist/cli.js
app_vs_agentic_layer.agentic_layer: src/core/audit, packaged/skills, scaffold/.github, and artifact renderers generate or ship agent surfaces

shared_state: process.env for provider auth and runtime feature flags
provider-scoped <cwd>/.claude|.agents|.pi/agentify state directories resolved by src/core/state-dir.ts
orchestrator agent/workflow event and cost files
greenfield CONTEXT.md, GOALS.md, docs and specs artifacts
shared_abstractions: src/core/ui/ — UI implementations used by public entrypoints

stable_types: CodebaseMapSchema property ordering and serialized shape are frozen by golden tests
WriteMapParamsSchema and WriteMapDeltaParamsSchema define tool ingress contracts
volatile_types: Artifact intents and optional evidence extensions evolve under compatibility-controlled schema additions

logging.pattern: Structured JSONL agentify.<event> records; summarize/redact/truncate before append

patterns.name: discriminated-state-classification
patterns.description: Expected filesystem/layout outcomes use literal-union statuses and rich inspection objects before command boundaries throw.

patterns.name: copy-verify-atomic-install
patterns.description: State migration journals durable phases, avoids symlinks, verifies fingerprints, atomically renames, and supports crash recovery.

patterns.name: repository-relative-path-hardening
patterns.description: Paths are normalized, checked against repository escape and symlink ancestors, then rejected on unsafe layouts.

patterns.name: structured-redacted-observability
patterns.description: Audit events are summarized, secret-redacted, truncated, serialized, and appended to run-scoped JSONL.

patterns.name: session-keyed-module-state
patterns.description: Audit runtime flags use an explicit session-keyed Map with cleanup to isolate concurrent sessions.

comment_style.docstring: JSDoc/doc comments explain public contracts and compatibility constraints
comment_style.inline: Comments explain safety rationale, transaction phases, or non-obvious behavior rather than syntax

conventions.test_naming: *.test.ts grouped under tests/ plus shell test-* scripts

pitfalls.what: Transaction cleanup is best-effort and can leave orphaned journal directories after filesystem errors.
pitfalls.consequence: Later runs may repeatedly enter interrupted-transaction recovery until the stale transaction is handled.

pitfalls.what: An explicit but unresolvable inherited primary model falls through silently to legacy provider/model resolution.
pitfalls.consequence: Explorer or lite sessions can unexpectedly use a different model without a warning at the pure resolver layer.

pitfalls.what: Generated user-facing files must be rendered from a validated, fully closed map rather than written during the audit.
pitfalls.consequence: Direct writes bypass schema, coverage, conflict, transaction, and user-ownership protections.

pitfalls.what: CodebaseMap schema property order and serialization are compatibility-tested contracts.
pitfalls.consequence: Reordering or casually changing schema composition breaks golden contract tests and persisted-map compatibility.

pitfalls.what: Pi does not auto-discover nested expert prompt directories.
pitfalls.consequence: Generated expert commands remain invisible unless the expert prompt path is added to Pi settings.

pitfalls.what: Provider-scoped state selection and retained-state migration are distinct; migration requires explicit targets and approval.
pitfalls.consequence: Treating legacy and provider-scoped trees as interchangeable risks ambiguous ownership or unsafe state movement.

spec_compliance_evidence: npm run test:package proves the packed npm artifact installs and the CLI runs
npm run test:parity verifies CLI, generated bundle, and state-directory contracts
npm run test:generated-output validates deterministic rendered surfaces and release evidence
npm run test:security-redteam exercises defense hardening and workflow simulation

ci_gates.name: package-smoke
ci_gates.command: npm run test:parity && npm run test:package && npm run test:maintenance && npm run test:generated-output && npm run test:security-redteam

env_vars_required: one configured provider credential or existing Pi OAuth credentials

deploy.target: npm registry from verified v*.*.* tag
deploy.command: npm publish from CI-packed tarball with provenance

gates: npm run test:all on Node 22.19 and 24
artifacts: npm-pack tarball retained by release workflow and published to npm

git_workflow.branch_naming: feat/, fix/, docs/, refactor/, ci/ descriptive branches

port_ranges.dev: none; published surface is a CLI
port_ranges.prod: 8787 is the loopback webhook subsystem default, not the primary CLI

commands: CLI exits nonzero on uncaught entrypoint errors
shutdown_procedure.fallback_behavior: No long-running primary app process; close embedded webhook server explicitly when used.

spawned_subprocesses.name: node tests/scripts/run-test-files.mjs
spawned_subprocesses.role: Discovers and executes TypeScript test files

spawned_subprocesses.name: npm run <script>
spawned_subprocesses.role: Build, test, parity, package, and release gate driver

env_var_purposes.provider API keys: Optional secret credential; one provider auth route is needed for model-backed runs
env_var_purposes.PI_BIN: Override Pi executable for expert subprocesses

port_determinism.range: No primary port; optional webhook defaults to loopback 8787.

package_json_scripts.test:all: npm run build && npm run test:generation-pipeline && npm run test:unit && bash tests/run.sh
package_json_scripts.test: npm run typecheck && npm run test:all
package_json_scripts.test:parity: npm run build && npm run test:parity:cli && npm run test:parity:generated && npm run test:parity:state && npm run test:package
package_json_scripts.test:maintenance: tsx tests/maintenance/documentation-invariants.test.ts && tsx tests/maintenance/schema-boundaries.test.ts && tsx tests/maintenance/reachability-invariants.test.ts && tsx tests/maintenance/module-boundaries.test.ts && tsx tests/maintenance/legacy-state-consumers.test.ts && tsx tests/release-safety.test.ts
package_json_scripts.test:generated-output: tsx tests/artifacts-renderers.test.ts && tsx tests/generated-output-quality.test.ts && tsx tests/core/expert-outcome.test.ts && tsx tests/core/smoke-evidence.test.ts && tsx tests/core/release-qualification.test.ts
package_json_scripts.release:check: npm run typecheck && npm run test:all && npm run test:package

fully_writable: provider-scoped state paths owned by Agentify
specs/** and ai_docs/** only when renderer ownership/conflict policy permits
src/** and tests/** only in explicitly write-capable implementation sessions

bash_blocked_patterns: compound shell operators (&&, ||, ;, pipes, substitutions, redirects)
permission/ownership escalation and system service mutation
curl/wget piped to interpreters or uploads
sudo, filesystem formatting, mount, reboot, device writes
env_allowlist: provider credential variable names selected by provider-auth.ts
damage_control_rules: Reject compound shell before command matching
Domain locks may narrow but never widen execution-policy roots
Audit and review sessions deny shell mutation
User-owned protected files cannot be overwritten
Script contents are re-scanned for blocked operations

tools: Audit: read, grep, find, ls plus trusted write_map/write_map_delta/spawn_explorer only
Write-capable sessions require explicit readable/writable roots and protected paths
commands: Pin npm, tsx, node, and bash invocations to known package scripts/files
Use execFile-style argv execution rather than shell parsing
paths: Normalize and repository-jail all tool paths
Deny zero-access patterns and ~/.agentify credential/config files
Honor protected user-owned files and per-session roots
env: Pass only provider-selected credential names where possible
Never log secret values; log safe key names only
Treat AGENTIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET and provider keys as production credentials
blocks: No inline interpreter evaluation or network-to-shell pipes
No secret reads, env dumps, privilege escalation, or external path access
logs: Redact provider keys, GitHub/AWS tokens, JWTs, PEM material, and authorization headers
Record tool allowlists, prompt hashes, map transitions, sub-agent dispatch, and external-path attempts

escape_hatch.blocking_unblock_path: Escalate through the host/user; do not weaken execution policy or defense hooks.

external_network_calls.purpose: Model-backed audit, explorer, greenfield, and expert sessions
Dependency install/audit and verified package publication
Optional GitHub runtime issue, PR, and workflow operations

D1_topography.evidence_summary: Root inventory and manifest identify real CLI entrypoints, source/test mirroring, generated boundaries, and five concrete orientation files.

D2_module_boundaries.evidence_summary: Bounded src analysis traced concrete CLI/import, audit/runtime RPC, and worker/state edges; provider-scoped state and common types/security/state-dir modules are shared abstractions, while audit, evals, engagement, webhook/AIW, and UI are separable subtrees.

D3_type_contract.evidence_summary: CodebaseMap is traced end-to-end from TypeBox schema/static type through bounded JSON parsing, validation, canonical persistence, coverage closure, and deterministic renderers; four concrete schema/type contracts and eight grep-able identifiers are recorded.

D4_conventions.evidence_summary: Production TypeScript uses kebab-case files, PascalCase contracts, verb-led camelCase functions, layered result/exception handling, structured redacted JSONL logging, explicit state parameters, and durable path/transaction patterns.

D5_pitfalls.evidence_summary: Six concrete footguns are source-referenced: orphaned transaction recovery, silent model fallback, renderer-only artifact writes, schema-order compatibility, nested expert discovery, and provider-state migration ambiguity.

D6_validation.evidence_summary: npm test is the aggregate gate; TypeScript checks, generation/unit/shell suites, parity, installed-package smoke, maintenance invariants, generated-output quality, security red-team, dependency audit, and CodeQL form layered validation; no standalone linter or browser E2E config exists.

D7_operational.evidence_summary: Node 22/npm builds with scripts/build.mjs, runs via node bin/agentify.js, publishes verified tags to npm, has a deep CI gate stack, optional provider credential envs, no primary app port, and an npm/tsconfig extension-ready environment.

D8_security.evidence_summary: Defense is layered through repository jail, zero-access secret paths, explicit session roots/tool sets, protected user ownership, compound/destructive command blocks, script rescanning, credential classification, structured redacted logs, and read-only external-trigger posture.

D9_process.evidence_summary: CONTRIBUTING, GitHub templates/workflows, CI, changelog policy, packaged review/document skills, and tag-only release docs define an issue/branch→implementation/test→PR review→documentation→verified release lifecycle.

D10_documentation.evidence_summary: Repository documentation is extensive: README, 113-line AGENTS guide, indexed architecture/security/state/release/operator docs, CONTRIBUTING and CHANGELOG; ai_docs/app_docs/specs/conditional-docs and existing Pi custom surfaces are honestly absent.

open_questions: Initial draft: gather repository evidence before closing coverage.

exploration_log.observation: Found a root product README, 113-line repository AGENTS guide, 24-file documentation tree/index, changelog and contributing/security docs; no ai_docs, app_docs, specs, conditional docs, or existing Pi extension/skill/prompt surfaces.
